verses.latin_vulgate: Veneruntque filii Israël et omnis multitudo in desertum Sin, mense primo, et mansit populus in Cades. Mortuaque est ibi Maria, et sepulta in eodem loco.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֣אוּ בְנֵֽי־יִ֠שְׂרָאֵ֠ל כׇּל־הָ֨עֵדָ֤ה מִדְבַּר־צִן֙ בַּחֹ֣דֶשׁ הָֽרִאשׁ֔וֹן וַיֵּ֥שֶׁב הָעָ֖ם בְּקָדֵ֑שׁ וַתָּ֤מׇת שָׁם֙ מִרְיָ֔ם וַתִּקָּבֵ֖ר שָֽׁם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the children of Israel, and all the multitude came into the desert of Sin, in the first month: and the people abode in Cades. And Mary died there, and was buried in the same place.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Israelin lapset tulivat kaiken seurakunnan kanssa Sinin korpeen, ensimäisellä kuulla, ja kansa viipyi Kadeksessa. Ja MirJam kuoli siellä, ja myös haudattiin sinne.
verses.king_james: Then came the children of Israel, even the whole congregation, into the desert of Zin in the first month: and the people abode in Kadesh; and Miriam died there, and was buried there.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque indigeret aqua populus, convenerunt adversum Moysen et Aaron :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹא־הָ֥יָה מַ֖יִם לָעֵדָ֑ה וַיִּקָּ֣הֲל֔וּ עַל־מֹשֶׁ֖ה וְעַֽל־אַהֲרֹֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the people wanting water, came together against Moses and Aaron:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kansalla ei ollut vettä, ja he kokoontuivat Mosesta ja Aaronia vastaan.
verses.king_james: And there was no water for the congregation: and they gathered themselves together against Moses and against Aaron.

verses.latin_vulgate: et versi in seditionem, dixerunt : Utinam periissemus inter fratres nostros coram Domino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֥רֶב הָעָ֖ם עִם־מֹשֶׁ֑ה וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ לֵאמֹ֔ר וְל֥וּ גָוַ֛עְנוּ בִּגְוַ֥ע אַחֵ֖ינוּ לִפְנֵ֥י יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And making a sedition, they said: Would God we had perished among our brethren before the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kansa riiteli Moseksen kanssa, ja sanoivat: jospa olisimme hukkuneet siellä, kussa meidän veljemme hukkuivat Herran edessä.
verses.king_james: And the people chode with Moses, and spake, saying, Would God that we had died when our brethren died before the Lord!

verses.latin_vulgate: Cur eduxistis ecclesiam Domini in solitudinem, ut et nos et nostra jumenta moriamur ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלָמָ֤ה הֲבֵאתֶם֙ אֶת־קְהַ֣ל יְהֹוָ֔ה אֶל־הַמִּדְבָּ֖ר הַזֶּ֑ה לָמ֣וּת שָׁ֔ם אֲנַ֖חְנוּ וּבְעִירֵֽנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Why have you brought out the church of the Lord into the wilderness, that both we and our cattle should die?
verses.finnish_biblia: Miksi olette vieneet tämän Herran Herran kansan tähän korpeen, kuolemaan täällä, sekä meitä että karjaamme.
verses.king_james: And why have ye brought up the congregation of the Lord into this wilderness, that we and our cattle should die there?

verses.latin_vulgate: quare nos fecistis ascendere de Ægypto, et adduxistis in locum istum pessimum, qui seri non potest, qui nec ficum gignit, nec vineas, nec malogranata, insuper et aquam non habet ad bibendum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלָמָ֤ה הֶֽעֱלִיתֻ֙נוּ֙ מִמִּצְרַ֔יִם לְהָבִ֣יא אֹתָ֔נוּ אֶל־הַמָּק֥וֹם הָרָ֖ע הַזֶּ֑ה לֹ֣א ׀ מְק֣וֹם זֶ֗רַע וּתְאֵנָ֤ה וְגֶ֙פֶן֙ וְרִמּ֔וֹן וּמַ֥יִם אַ֖יִן לִשְׁתּֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Why have you made us come up out of Egypt, and have brought us into this wretched place which cannot be sowed, nor bringeth forth figs, nor vines, nor pomegranates, neither is there any water to drink?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja miksi olette johdattaneet meidät Egyptistä, tuodaksenne meitä tähän pahaan paikkaan, jossa emme taida kylvää, jossa ei myös ole fikunia, taikka viinapuita, ei myös granatin omenia, eipä ole vettäkään juodaksemme?
verses.king_james: And wherefore have ye made us to come up out of Egypt, to bring us in unto this evil place? it is no place of seed, or of figs, or of vines, or of pomegranates; neither is there any water to drink.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ingressusque Moyses et Aaron, dimissa multitudine, tabernaculum fœderis, corruerunt proni in terram, clamaveruntque ad Dominum, atque dixerunt : Domine Deus, audi clamorem hujus populi, et aperi eis thesaurum tuum fontem aquæ vivæ, ut satiati, cesset murmuratio eorum. Et apparuit gloria Domini super eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹא֩ מֹשֶׁ֨ה וְאַהֲרֹ֜ן מִפְּנֵ֣י הַקָּהָ֗ל אֶל־פֶּ֙תַח֙ אֹ֣הֶל מוֹעֵ֔ד וַֽיִּפְּל֖וּ עַל־פְּנֵיהֶ֑ם וַיֵּרָ֥א כְבוֹד־יְהֹוָ֖ה אֲלֵיהֶֽם׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And Moses and Aaron leaving the multitude, went into the tabernacle of the covenant, and fell flat upon the ground, and cried to the Lord, and said. O Lord God, hear the cry of this people, and open to them thy treasure, a fountain of living water, that being satisfied, they may cease to murmur. And the glory of the Lord appeared over them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Moses ja Aaron menivät kansan tyköä seurakunnan majan ovelle ja lankesivat kasvoillensa, ja Herran kunnia näkyi heille.
verses.king_james: And Moses and Aaron went from the presence of the assembly unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and they fell upon their faces: and the glory of the Lord appeared unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Locutusque est Dominus ad Moysen, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְדַבֵּ֥ר יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶל־מֹשֶׁ֥ה לֵּאמֹֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra puhui Mosekselle, sanoen:
verses.king_james: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Tolle virgam, et congrega populum, tu et Aaron frater tuus, et loquimini ad petram coram eis, et illa dabit aquas. Cumque eduxeris aquam de petra, bibet omnis multitudo et jumenta ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קַ֣ח אֶת־הַמַּטֶּ֗ה וְהַקְהֵ֤ל אֶת־הָעֵדָה֙ אַתָּה֙ וְאַהֲרֹ֣ן אָחִ֔יךָ וְדִבַּרְתֶּ֧ם אֶל־הַסֶּ֛לַע לְעֵינֵיהֶ֖ם וְנָתַ֣ן מֵימָ֑יו וְהוֹצֵאתָ֨ לָהֶ֥ם מַ֙יִם֙ מִן־הַסֶּ֔לַע וְהִשְׁקִיתָ֥ אֶת־הָעֵדָ֖ה וְאֶת־בְּעִירָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Take the rod, and assemble the people together, thou and Aaron thy brother, and speak to the rock before them, and it shall yield waters. And when thou hast brought forth water out of the rock, all the multitude and their cattle shall drink.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ota sauva, ja kokoa kansa, sinä ja veljes Aaron, ja puhukaat kalliolle heidän silmäinsä edessä, ja se antaa vetensä. Ja näin sinun pitää saattaman heille vettä kalliosta, ja juottaman kansan ja heidän karjansa.
verses.king_james: Take the rod, and gather thou the assembly together, thou, and Aaron thy brother, and speak ye unto the rock before their eyes; and it shall give forth his water, and thou shalt bring forth to them water out of the rock: so thou shalt give the congregation and their beasts drink.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tulit igitur Moyses virgam, quæ erat in conspectu Domini, sicut præceperat ei,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַּ֥ח מֹשֶׁ֛ה אֶת־הַמַּטֶּ֖ה מִלִּפְנֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֑ה כַּאֲשֶׁ֖ר צִוָּֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Moses therefore took the rod, which was before the Lord, as he had commanded him,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin otti Moses sauvan Herran edestä, niinkuin hän käski hänelle.
verses.king_james: And Moses took the rod from before the Lord, as he commanded him.

verses.latin_vulgate: congregata multitudine ante petram : dixitque eis : Audite, rebelles et increduli : num de petra hac vobis aquam poterimus ejicere ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּקְהִ֜לוּ מֹשֶׁ֧ה וְאַהֲרֹ֛ן אֶת־הַקָּהָ֖ל אֶל־פְּנֵ֣י הַסָּ֑לַע וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לָהֶ֗ם שִׁמְעוּ־נָא֙ הַמֹּרִ֔ים הֲמִן־הַסֶּ֣לַע הַזֶּ֔ה נוֹצִ֥יא לָכֶ֖ם מָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And having gathered together the multitude before the rock, he said to them: Hear, ye rebellious and incredulous: Can we bring you forth water out of this rock?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Moses ja Aaron kokosivat kansan kallion eteen, ja sanoivat heille: kuulkaat nyt te niskurit: pitääkö meidän tästä kalliosta teille vettä saaman?
verses.king_james: And Moses and Aaron gathered the congregation together before the rock, and he said unto them, Hear now, ye rebels; must we fetch you water out of this rock?

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque elevasset Moyses manum, percutiens virga bis silicem, egressæ sunt aquæ largissimæ, ita ut populus biberet et jumenta.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֨רֶם מֹשֶׁ֜ה אֶת־יָד֗וֹ וַיַּ֧ךְ אֶת־הַסֶּ֛לַע בְּמַטֵּ֖הוּ פַּעֲמָ֑יִם וַיֵּצְאוּ֙ מַ֣יִם רַבִּ֔ים וַתֵּ֥שְׁתְּ הָעֵדָ֖ה וּבְעִירָֽם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And when Moses bad lifted up his hand, and struck the rock twice with the rod, there came forth water in great abundance, so that the people and their cattle drank,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Moses nosti kätensä ja löi kahdesti sauvallansa kallioon, niin juoksi siitä paljo vettä, ja kansa ja heidän karjansa saivat juoda.
verses.king_james: And Moses lifted up his hand, and with his rod he smote the rock twice: and the water came out abundantly, and the congregation drank, and their beasts also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Dominus ad Moysen et Aaron : Quia non credidistis mihi, ut sanctificaretis me coram filiis Israël, non introducetis hos populos in terram, quam dabo eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר יְהֹוָה֮ אֶל־מֹשֶׁ֣ה וְאֶֽל־אַהֲרֹן֒ יַ֚עַן לֹא־הֶאֱמַנְתֶּ֣ם בִּ֔י לְהַ֨קְדִּישֵׁ֔נִי לְעֵינֵ֖י בְּנֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל לָכֵ֗ן לֹ֤א תָבִ֙יאוּ֙ אֶת־הַקָּהָ֣ל הַזֶּ֔ה אֶל־הָאָ֖רֶץ אֲשֶׁר־נָתַ֥תִּי לָהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord said to Moses and Aaron: Because you have not believed me, to sanctify me before the children of Israel, you shall not bring these people into the land, which I will give them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra sanoi Mosekselle ja Aaronille: ettette uskoneet minun päälleni, pyhittääksenne minua Israelin lasten edessä, ei teidän pidä johdattaman tätä joukkoa siihen maahan, jonka minä heille annan.
verses.king_james: And the Lord spake unto Moses and Aaron, Because ye believed me not, to sanctify me in the eyes of the children of Israel, therefore ye shall not bring this congregation into the land which I have given them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc est aqua contradictionis, ubi jurgati sunt filii Israël contra Dominum, et sanctificatus est in eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֚מָּה מֵ֣י מְרִיבָ֔ה אֲשֶׁר־רָב֥וּ בְנֵֽי־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל אֶת־יְהֹוָ֑ה וַיִּקָּדֵ֖שׁ בָּֽם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: This is the Water of contradiction, where the children of Israel strove with words against the Lord, and he was sanctified in them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tämä on se riitavesi, josta Israelin lapset riitelivät Herran kanssa: ja hän pyhitettiin heidän seassansa.
verses.king_james: This is the water of Meribah; because the children of Israel strove with the Lord, and he was sanctified in them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misit interea nuntios Moyses de Cades ad regem Edom, qui dicerent : Hæc mandat frater tuus Israël : Nosti omnem laborem qui apprehendit nos,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֨ח מֹשֶׁ֧ה מַלְאָכִ֛ים מִקָּדֵ֖שׁ אֶל־מֶ֣לֶךְ אֱד֑וֹם כֹּ֤ה אָמַר֙ אָחִ֣יךָ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אַתָּ֣ה יָדַ֔עְתָּ אֵ֥ת כׇּל־הַתְּלָאָ֖ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר מְצָאָֽתְנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the mean time Moses sent messengers from Cades to the king of Edom, to say: Thus saith thy brother Israel: Thou knowest all the labour that hath come upon us:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Moses lähetti sanansaattajat Kadeksesta Edomin kuninkaan tykö, (sanoen:) näin sanoo veljes Israel: sinä tiedät kaiken vaivan, joka meille on tapahtunut,
verses.king_james: And Moses sent messengers from Kadesh unto the king of Edom, Thus saith thy brother Israel, Thou knowest all the travail that hath befallen us:

verses.latin_vulgate: quomodo descenderint patres nostri in Ægyptum, et habitaverimus ibi multo tempore, afflixerintque nos Ægyptii, et patres nostros :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּרְד֤וּ אֲבֹתֵ֙ינוּ֙ מִצְרַ֔יְמָה וַנֵּ֥שֶׁב בְּמִצְרַ֖יִם יָמִ֣ים רַבִּ֑ים וַיָּרֵ֥עוּ לָ֛נוּ מִצְרַ֖יִם וְלַאֲבֹתֵֽינוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: In what manner our fathers went down into Egypt, and there we dwelt a long time, and the Egyptians afflicted us and our fathers.
verses.finnish_biblia: Että meidän isämme menivät alas Egyptiin ja me asuimme Egyptissä pitkän ajan, ja Egyptiläiset vaivasivat meitä ja meidän isiämme,
verses.king_james: How our fathers went down into Egypt, and we have dwelt in Egypt a long time; and the Egyptians vexed us, and our fathers:

verses.latin_vulgate: et quomodo clamaverimus ad Dominum, et exaudierit nos, miseritque angelum, qui eduxerit nos de Ægypto. Ecce in urbe Cades, quæ est in extremis finibus tuis, positi,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַנִּצְעַ֤ק אֶל־יְהֹוָה֙ וַיִּשְׁמַ֣ע קֹלֵ֔נוּ וַיִּשְׁלַ֣ח מַלְאָ֔ךְ וַיֹּצִאֵ֖נוּ מִמִּצְרָ֑יִם וְהִנֵּה֙ אֲנַ֣חְנוּ בְקָדֵ֔שׁ עִ֖יר קְצֵ֥ה גְבוּלֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And how we cried to the Lord, and he heard us, and sent an angel, who hath brought us out of Egypt. Lo, we are now in the city of Cades, which is in the uttermost of thy borders,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja me huusimme Herran tykö, ja hän kuuli äänemme, ja lähetti Enkelin, ja vei meidät Egyptistä ulos. Ja katso, me olemme Kadeksessa, siinä kaupungissa, joka on sinun äärimäisillä rajoillas.
verses.king_james: And when we cried unto the Lord, he heard our voice, and sent an angel, and hath brought us forth out of Egypt: and, behold, we are in Kadesh, a city in the uttermost of thy border:

verses.latin_vulgate: obsecramus ut nobis transire liceat per terram tuam. Non ibimus per agros, nec per vineas ; non bibemus aquas de puteis tuis : sed gradiemur via publica, nec ad dexteram nec ad sinistram declinantes, donec transeamus terminos tuos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נַעְבְּרָה־נָּ֣א בְאַרְצֶ֗ךָ לֹ֤א נַעֲבֹר֙ בְּשָׂדֶ֣ה וּבְכֶ֔רֶם וְלֹ֥א נִשְׁתֶּ֖ה מֵ֣י בְאֵ֑ר דֶּ֧רֶךְ הַמֶּ֣לֶךְ נֵלֵ֗ךְ לֹ֤א נִטֶּה֙ יָמִ֣ין וּשְׂמֹ֔אול עַ֥ד אֲשֶֽׁר־נַעֲבֹ֖ר גְּבֻלֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And we beseech thee that we may have leave to pass through thy country. We will not go through the fields, nor through the vineyards, we will not drink the waters of thy wells, but we will go by the common highway, neither turning aside to the right hand, nor to the left, till we are past thy borders.
verses.finnish_biblia: Anna meidän vaeltaa maas lävitse: emme poikkee peltoihin eli viinamäkiin, emme myös juo vettä kaivoista; me vaellamme oikiaa maantietä, ei poiketen oikialle eikä vasemmalle puolelle, siihenasti kuin tulemme sinun rajas ylitse.
verses.king_james: Let us pass, I pray thee, through thy country: we will not pass through the fields, or through the vineyards, neither will we drink of the water of the wells: we will go by the king’s high way, we will not turn to the right hand nor to the left, until we have passed thy borders.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui respondit Edom : Non transibis per me, alioquin armatus occurram tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אֵלָיו֙ אֱד֔וֹם לֹ֥א תַעֲבֹ֖ר בִּ֑י פֶּן־בַּחֶ֖רֶב אֵצֵ֥א לִקְרָאתֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Edom answered them: Thou shalt not pass by me: if thou dost I will come out armed against thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Edom sanoi hänelle: ei sinun pidä minun kauttani vaeltaman, taikka minä kohtaan sinua miekalla.
verses.king_james: And Edom said unto him, Thou shalt not pass by me, lest I come out against thee with the sword.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixeruntque filii Israël : Per tritam gradiemur viam : et si biberimus aquas tuas, nos et pecora nostra, dabimus quod justum est : nulla erit in pretio difficultas, tantum velociter transeamus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמְר֨וּ אֵלָ֥יו בְּנֵֽי־יִשְׂרָאֵל֮ בַּֽמְסִלָּ֣ה נַעֲלֶה֒ וְאִם־מֵימֶ֤יךָ נִשְׁתֶּה֙ אֲנִ֣י וּמִקְנַ֔י וְנָתַתִּ֖י מִכְרָ֑ם רַ֥ק אֵין־דָּבָ֖ר בְּרַגְלַ֥י אֶֽעֱבֹֽרָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the children of Israel said: We will go by the beaten way: and if we and our cattle drink of thy waters, we will give thee what is just: there shall be no difficulty in the price, only let us pass speedily.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Israelin lapset sanoivat hänelle: me vaellamme yhteistä maantietä, ja jos me ja meidän karjamme juovat sinun vettäs, niin me sen maksamme, emme ilman mitään tahdo; ainoastansa jalkaisin vaellamme sen lävitse.
verses.king_james: And the children of Israel said unto him, We will go by the high way: and if I and my cattle drink of thy water, then I will pay for it: I will only, without doing any thing else, go through on my feet.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille respondit : Non transibis. Statimque egressus est obvius, cum infinita multitudo, et manu forti,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֖אמֶר לֹ֣א תַעֲבֹ֑ר וַיֵּצֵ֤א אֱדוֹם֙ לִקְרָאת֔וֹ בְּעַ֥ם כָּבֵ֖ד וּבְיָ֥ד חֲזָקָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But he answered: Thou shalt not pass. And immediately he came forth to meet them with an infinite multitude, and a strong hand,
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta hän sanoi: ei sinun pidä vaeltaman tästä lävitse. Ja Edomilaiset läksivät heitä vastaan paljolla väellä ja väkevällä kädellä.
verses.king_james: And he said, Thou shalt not go through. And Edom came out against him with much people, and with a strong hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: nec voluit acquiescere deprecanti, ut concederet transitum per fines suos. Quam ob rem divertit ab eo Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְמָאֵ֣ן ׀ אֱד֗וֹם נְתֹן֙ אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל עֲבֹ֖ר בִּגְבֻל֑וֹ וַיֵּ֥ט יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל מֵעָלָֽיו׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Neither would he condescend to their desire to grant them passage through his borders. Wherefore Israel turned another way from him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja näin estivät Edomilaiset Israelin vaeltamasta maansa äären lävitse. Ja Israel käänsi itsensä heistä pois.
verses.king_james: Thus Edom refused to give Israel passage through his border: wherefore Israel turned away from him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque castra movissent de Cades, venerunt in montem Hor, qui est in finibus terræ Edom :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּסְע֖וּ מִקָּדֵ֑שׁ וַיָּבֹ֧אוּ בְנֵֽי־יִשְׂרָאֵ֛ל כׇּל־הָעֵדָ֖ה הֹ֥ר הָהָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when they had removed the camp from Cades, they came to mount Hor, which is in the borders of the land of Edom:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Israelin lapset läksivät Kadeksesta ja tulivat kaiken joukon kanssa Horin vuoren tykö.
verses.king_james: And the children of Israel, even the whole congregation, journeyed from Kadesh, and came unto mount Hor.

verses.latin_vulgate: ubi locutus est Dominus ad Moysen :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֧אמֶר יְהֹוָ֛ה אֶל־מֹשֶׁ֥ה וְאֶֽל־אַהֲרֹ֖ן בְּהֹ֣ר הָהָ֑ר עַל־גְּב֥וּל אֶֽרֶץ־אֱד֖וֹם לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Where the Lord spoke to Moses:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra puhui Mosekselle ja Aaronille Horin vuorella, Edomin maan rajalla, ja sanoi:
verses.king_james: And the Lord spake unto Moses and Aaron in mount Hor, by the coast of the land of Edom, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Pergat, inquit, Aaron ad populos suos : non enim intrabit terram, quam dedi filiis Israël, eo quod incredulus fuerit ori meo, ad aquas contradictionis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֵאָסֵ֤ף אַהֲרֹן֙ אֶל־עַמָּ֔יו כִּ֣י לֹ֤א יָבֹא֙ אֶל־הָאָ֔רֶץ אֲשֶׁ֥ר נָתַ֖תִּי לִבְנֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל עַ֛ל אֲשֶׁר־מְרִיתֶ֥ם אֶת־פִּ֖י לְמֵ֥י מְרִיבָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let Aaron, saith he, go to his people: for he shall not go into the land which I have given the children of Israel, because he was incredulous to my words, at the waters of contradiction.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kokoontukaan Aaron kansansa tykö; sillä ei hänen pidä tuleman siihen maahan, jonka minä Israelin lapsille antanut olen, että te olitte minun suulleni kovakorvaiset riitaveden tykönä.
verses.king_james: Aaron shall be gathered unto his people: for he shall not enter into the land which I have given unto the children of Israel, because ye rebelled against my word at the water of Meribah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tolle Aaron et filium ejus cum eo, et duces eos in montem Hor.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קַ֚ח אֶֽת־אַהֲרֹ֔ן וְאֶת־אֶלְעָזָ֖ר בְּנ֑וֹ וְהַ֥עַל אֹתָ֖ם הֹ֥ר הָהָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Take Aaron and his son with him, and bring them up into mount Hor:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin ota siis Aaron ja hänen poikansa Eleatsar, ja vie heidät Horin vuorelle.
verses.king_james: Take Aaron and Eleazar his son, and bring them up unto mount Hor:

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque nudaveris patrem veste sua, indues ea Eleazarum filium ejus : Aaron colligetur, et morietur ibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַפְשֵׁ֤ט אֶֽת־אַהֲרֹן֙ אֶת־בְּגָדָ֔יו וְהִלְבַּשְׁתָּ֖ם אֶת־אֶלְעָזָ֣ר בְּנ֑וֹ וְאַהֲרֹ֥ן יֵאָסֵ֖ף וּמֵ֥ת שָֽׁם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when thou hast stripped the father of his vesture, thou shalt vest therewith Eleazar his son: Aaron shall be gathered to his people, and die there.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja riisu Aaronin yltä vaatteet, puettaakses hänen poikansa Eleatsarin ylle: ja Aaron pitää siellä koottaman ja kuoleman.
verses.king_james: And strip Aaron of his garments, and put them upon Eleazar his son: and Aaron shall be gathered unto his people, and shall die there.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fecit Moyses ut præceperat Dominus : et ascenderunt in montem Hor coram omni multitudine.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֣עַשׂ מֹשֶׁ֔ה כַּאֲשֶׁ֖ר צִוָּ֣ה יְהֹוָ֑ה וַֽיַּעֲלוּ֙ אֶל־הֹ֣ר הָהָ֔ר לְעֵינֵ֖י כׇּל־הָעֵדָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Moses did as the Lord had commanded: and they went up into mount Hor before all the multitude.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Moses teki niinkuin Herra hänen käski, ja he astuivat Horin vuorelle kaiken kansan nähden.
verses.king_james: And Moses did as the Lord commanded: and they went up into mount Hor in the sight of all the congregation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque Aaron spoliasset vestibus suis, induit eis Eleazarum filium ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּפְשֵׁט֩ מֹשֶׁ֨ה אֶֽת־אַהֲרֹ֜ן אֶת־בְּגָדָ֗יו וַיַּלְבֵּ֤שׁ אֹתָם֙ אֶת־אֶלְעָזָ֣ר בְּנ֔וֹ וַיָּ֧מׇת אַהֲרֹ֛ן שָׁ֖ם בְּרֹ֣אשׁ הָהָ֑ר וַיֵּ֧רֶד מֹשֶׁ֛ה וְאֶלְעָזָ֖ר מִן־הָהָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when he had stripped Aaron of his vestments, he vested Eleazar his son with them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Moses riisui Aaronilta hänen vaatteensa, ja puetti hänen poikansa Eleatsarin ylle. Ja Aaron kuoli siellä, vuoren kukkulalla, mutta Moses ja Eleatsar astuivat vuorelta alas.
verses.king_james: And Moses stripped Aaron of his garments, and put them upon Eleazar his son; and Aaron died there in the top of the mount: and Moses and Eleazar came down from the mount.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis autem multitudo videns occubuisse Aaron, flevit super eo triginta diebus per cunctas familias suas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּרְאוּ֙ כׇּל־הָ֣עֵדָ֔ה כִּ֥י גָוַ֖ע אַהֲרֹ֑ן וַיִּבְכּ֤וּ אֶֽת־אַהֲרֹן֙ שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים י֔וֹם כֹּ֖ל בֵּ֥ית יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And all the multitude seeing that Aaron was dead, mourned for him thirty days throughout all their families.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja koska kaikki kansa näki Aaronin kuolleeksi, itki koko Israelin huone häntä kolmekymmentä päivää.
verses.king_james: And when all the congregation saw that Aaron was dead, they mourned for Aaron thirty days, even all the house of Israel.
